title: Big sums are safe to hold
subtitle: A present-tense affirmation for claiming that large amounts of money are stable, safe, and right to keep.
excerpt: A short affirmation practice from Day 27 of the MONEY program. Use when the nervous system responds to incoming wealth as though it must immediately be spent, given away, or dispersed.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 27 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

content.text: An affirmation from Day 27 of the MONEY program. The word "safe" is specific: this is about the nervous system's assessment of holding wealth, not a moral statement. Big sums are not dangerous. They do not need to be immediately dispersed. They are safe to hold.
Big sums are safe to hold.
Say it out loud three times, hand on heart. If you notice the urge to add a qualifier ("as long as I'm careful" or "once I know what to do with it"), that's the part to work with.
Adapted from Day 27 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
